flows.name: @relay/flows-codebase-discovery
flows.description: Explores a codebase and produces an HTML report covering packages, entities, services, and architecture.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
codebase-discovery

The canonical reference flow. Point it at any repository and it produces a single self-contained HTML report describing the code that lives there — which packages exist, which entities and services they expose, and how the pieces fit together.

What it does

Four prompt steps, arranged as a fan-out over a shared inventory. The first step () walks the repository and emits a JSON handoff listing every package, its language, and its top-level structure. Two steps then run in par

flows.name: @relay/example-git-log-summary
flows.description: A two-step example flow that demonstrates step.script(): capture 20 recent git commits, then ask Claude to write a short changelog entry.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
git-log-summary

●─▶●─▶●─▶●  git-log-summary

A minimal script-then-prompt example. Use it to see how  captures shell output into a run artifact, then hands the run off to a prompt step that turns the data into a markdown document.

Purpose

The flow captures the 20 most recent commits on the current branch with , then asks Claude to read that capture and write a short changelog entry as a markdown artifact. It exists to demonstrate the script-then-prompt pattern — most real flows start with a s

flows.name: @relay/example-file-type-router
flows.description: A branch-step example flow that routes a file path to one of three review prompts based on its extension — .ts, .js, or other.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
file-type-router

●─▶●─▶●─▶●  file-type-router

A routing example. Use it to see how  inspects a condition, maps an exit code to a step id, and selects exactly one downstream step out of a fan — the pattern most real flows use for dispatching on file kind, feature flag, or input shape.

Purpose

The flow looks at one file path and picks a review prompt that matches the file's extension:

- .ts files go to a TypeScript-specific code review.
- .js files go to a JavaScript-specific code review.
- A

flows.name: @relay/example-multi-perspective-review
flows.description: A five-step example flow that demonstrates step.parallel(): three reviewers read the same file in parallel (security, performance, readability), then an aggregation step synthesizes their handoffs into a single report.
flows.readmeExcerpt:
multi-perspective-review

●─▶●─▶●─▶●  multi-perspective-review

A five-step fan-out / fan-in example. Use it to see how  dispatches three independent reviewers against the same input and how a downstream step collects their handoffs into a single report.

Purpose

Three reviewers read the same source file at the same time — one looks for security issues, one for performance issues, one for readability issues. Each reviewer writes a typed JSON handoff. A final aggregation step reads all three han
